title: Python TUI native LICHEN client with LilyGO-style UI
description: Why this exists: LICHEN needs a first-party native client that works from developer laptops and field computers without requiring a mobile app. The Python TUI should exercise the native app contract directly and provide a practical operator interface for messaging, node status, configuration, mesh state, logs, and diagnostics. The visual design should approximate the LilyGO built-in device UI as closely as a terminal UI reasonably can: compact, device-like, status-bar driven, message-focused, and usable on small terminal windows. What needs to be done: build a Python TUI client with two native connection paths, one over IP/CoAP and one over direct BLE, sharing the same app-level behavior and data model. Acceptance criteria: client can connect over BLE and IP/CoAP where supported; screens cover pairing/connection, inbox/thread view, compose/send, node/status, mesh neighbors, config basics, logs, and diagnostics; UI reference notes identify which LilyGO built-in UI is being matched; visual tests or terminal snapshots cover core screens; package entry point and docs exist; code review and user smoke test notes are recorded before closure.
notes: Design constraint from 2026-06-30: the Python TUI should look as much as possible like the LilyGO built-in UI while staying terminal-native and avoiding unlicensed asset copying. It should also serve as a reference native client for both IP/CoAP and BLE paths.
